texto: One foot in front of the other, more aimless than direct, Bradford left the waiting room for the outside world. Called for a taxi and then dialed Munroe again, desperate for her voice, for one ray of light in the darkness, afraid of what he might say if she did answer, afraid of himself and the inner deadening that pointed to a danger far more lethal than any rage he'd felt.

texto: The pain on the inside is what keeps you human,' she said. 'Never forget that.

texto: But people like the doll guy who sells women and the dog guy who buys women, and other guys who, say, rape women, or maybe don’t go as far as violent rape but treat women like objects instead of people—sure, there’s a difference in the level of crime, but it’s all the same thing, where women become a canvas for throwing emotional baggage, Jackson Pollock style.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Taylor Stevens: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “But people like the doll guy who sells women and the dog guy who buys women, and other guys who, …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sucesso, crime, rape pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de rape e violence.

texto: Lumani had never managed a failed delivery because, in the end, no matter how skilled or how hard they fought back, pressure applied in the right places caused even the strongest men to fracture.But this one? He'd watched her. Studied her. Observed what maybe even Uncle, the reader of people, had missed. This one was already fractured, and the lines between her broken pieces were not fissures but scar material stronger than whatever had once filled those spaces.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Taylor Stevens que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “Lumani had never managed a failed delivery because, in the end, no matter how skilled or how hard…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre vida, broken, damaged, com foco em termino e damaged.

texto: This doll-guy situation is an extreme of what I deal with in everyday life, where men believe that what they want I want, and they project that on to me and then blame me, curse me, when I don’t respond the way they’ve fantasized, like it’s some personal attack on them, like they’re entitled to something. Doll guy and dog guy and rape guy, the dangerous ones, they just go a step further and take it anyway. Then they blame you and the way you look for what they did. What’s worse is that a lot of the time, society blames you, too.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Taylor Stevens: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “This doll-guy situation is an extreme of what I deal with in everyday life, where men believe tha…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, crime, rape pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de rape e violence.
